summary: Jest snapshot tests are failing because thrown Babel parsing errors do not match stored error message snapshots. Multiple error-format tests failed on toThrowErrorMatchingSnapshot() assertions.
root_cause: Snapshot assertion failures in tests/format/typescript/_errors_/modifiers/format.test.js and tests/format/js/_errors_/import/format.test.js. The expect().rejects.toThrowErrorMatchingSnapshot() matcher detected mismatches between actual Babel parser errors and stored snapshot expectations.

evidence.reason: Direct evidence of snapshot matcher failure; the thrown error does not match the stored snapshot
Stack trace explicitly identifies the failing assertion type as snapshot matching
Test failure confirmed in error-formatting test suite (_errors_ subdirectory indicates these test error messages)

evidence.quote: SyntaxError: A parameter initializer is only allowed in a functi...
evidence.reason: Actual Babel error thrown; differs from what the snapshot expects
Babel parser error creation in stack trace shows real error being thrown is not matching stored snapshot

suggested_fix: Review the snapshot files for tests/format/typescript/_errors_/modifiers/format.test.js and tests/format/js/_errors_/import/format.test.js to determine if Babel error message formatting has changed, then update snapshots accordingly or revert any changes that altered error output.
